configurations.name: with_skill
configurations.label: Fixed Skills v1.5 (correct params + string-typed booleans + defaults)

configurations.name: old_skill
configurations.label: Baseline Skills v1.2 (wrong MCP parameter names, no advisor params)

observations: Fixed skills v1.5 score 16/17 (94.1%) vs baseline v1.2 at 11/17 (64.7%) — a +29.4% improvement.
5 discriminating assertions all pass with fixed skills, all fail with baseline:
- uses_impact_not_severity: v1.5 uses impact='5,7' (correct numeric IDs). v1.2 uses severity='Critical' (nonexistent param).
- uses_correct_param_names: v1.5 uses advisory_available, impact. v1.2 uses affecting, severity (both nonexistent).
- uses_advisory_available: v1.5 uses advisory_available='true' as string. v1.2 uses affecting=true (wrong name + wrong type).
- uses_impacting_as_string: v1.5 explicitly passes impacting='true' (string). v1.2 doesn't use impacting at all (in production, LLM infers it from schema and passes boolean True).
- uses_correct_inventory_params: v1.5 uses per_page=10, page=1 (correct). v1.2 uses limit=20 (wrong param name).
1 non-discriminating failure (both fail): flags_stale_checkins — neither version flags systems with stale check-ins, even though guardrails-safety instructs it.
Eval 5 (advisor) now has 6 assertions (was 4 in iteration-4). The new uses_impacting_as_string assertion is the key discriminator for the advisor boolean bug.
Root cause confirmed: v1.2 skills document wrong parameter names and lack advisor parameter guidance, causing production tool call failures.
qualitative_differences: Eval 4: v1.5 uses impact='5,7' for single-call multi-severity. v1.2 uses severity='Critical' which doesn't exist in the MCP schema — the tool call would fail in production.
Eval 4: v1.5 uses advisory_available='true' (string). v1.2 uses affecting=true (wrong name + boolean type) — silently ignored by MCP server.

Eval 5: v1.5 explicitly uses impacting='true' (string-typed boolean). v1.2 omits impacting entirely — in production the LLM infers it from schema and passes boolean True, causing intermittent failures.
Eval 6: v1.5 uses per_page=10, page=1 (correct MCP params). v1.2 uses limit=20 (wrong param, silently ignored by MCP server).
Eval 6: Both versions sort by display_name — the old response-formatting skill already had this guidance.
